volumeTitle: Children of Heracles. Hippolytus. Andromache. Hecuba
label: Hippolytus (Section 902 - Section 1084)

sections.label: Section 902
sections.original: κραυγῆς ἀκούσας σῆς ἀφικόμην , πάτερ ,
sections.translation: I heard thy voice , father , and hasted to come hither ; yet know I not the cause of thy present [*] sorrow , but would fain learn of thee .

sections.label: Section 905
sections.original: ἔα , τί χρῆμα ; σὴν δάμαρθ ’ ὁρῶ , πάτερ ,
sections.translation: Ha ! what is this ? thy wife a corpse I see ; this is passing strange ; ’ twas but now I left her ; a moment since she looked upon the light .

sections.label: Section 909
sections.original: τί χρῆμα πάσχει ; τῷ τρόπῳ διόλλυται ;
sections.translation: How came she thus ? the manner of her death ?

sections.label: Section 910
sections.original: πάτερ , πυθέσθαι βούλομαι σέθεν πάρα .
sections.translation: this would I learn of thee , father . Art dumb ? silence availeth not in trouble ; nay , for the heart that fain would know all must show its curiosity even in sorrow ’ s hour . Be sure it is not right , father ,

sections.label: Section 915
sections.original: κρύπτειν δίκαιον σάς , πάτερ , δυσπραξίας .
sections.translation: to hide misfortunes from those who love , ay , more than love thee .

sections.label: Section 916
sections.original: ὦ πόλλ ’ ἁμαρτάνοντες ἄνθρωποι μάτην ,
sections.translation: O ye sons of men , victims of a thousand idle errors , why teach your countless crafts , why scheme and seek to find a way for everything , while one thing ye know not nor ever yet have made your prize ,

sections.label: Section 920
sections.original: φρονεῖν διδάσκειν οἷσιν οὐκ ἔνεστι νοῦς ;
sections.translation: a way to teach them wisdom whose souls are void of sense ?

sections.label: Section 921
sections.original: δεινὸν σοφιστὴν εἶπας , ὅστις εὖ φρονεῖν
sections.translation: A very master in his craft the man , who can force fools to be wise ! But these ill-timed subtleties of thine , father , make me fear thy tongue is running riot through trouble .

sections.label: Section 925
sections.original: φεῦ , χρῆν βροτοῖσι τῶν φίλων τεκμήριον
sections.translation: Fie upon thee ! man needs should have some certain test set up to try his friends , some touchstone of their hearts , to know each friend whether he be true or false ; all men should have two voices , one the voice of honesty , expediency ’ s the other ,

sections.label: Section 930
sections.original: ὡς ἡ φρονοῦσα τἄδικ ’ ἐξηλέγχετο
sections.translation: so would honesty confute its knavish opposite , and then we could not be deceived .

sections.label: Section 932
sections.original: ἀλλ ’ ἦ τις ἐς σὸν οὖς με διαβαλὼν ἔχει
sections.translation: Say , hath some friend been slandering me and hath he still thine ear ? am I , though guiltless , banned ? I am amazed indeed ;

sections.label: Section 935
sections.original: λόγοι παραλλάσσοντες ἔξεδροι φρενῶν .
sections.translation: thy random , frantic words fill me with wild alarm .

sections.label: Section 936
sections.original: φεῦ τῆς βροτείας · ποῖ προβήσεται ; φρενός .
sections.translation: O the mind of mortal man ! to what lengths will it proceed ? What limit will its bold assurance have ? for if it goes on growing as man ’ s life advances , and each successor outdo the man before him

sections.label: Section 940
sections.original: πανοῦργος ἔσται , θεοῖσι προσβαλεῖν χθονὶ
sections.translation: in villainy , the gods will have to add another sphere unto the world , which shall take in the knaves and villains .

sections.label: Section 943
sections.original: σκέψασθε δ ’ ἐς τόνδ ’, ὅστις ἐξ ἐμοῦ γεγὼς
sections.translation: Behold this man ; he , my own son , hath outraged mine honour , his guilt most clearly proved

sections.label: Section 945
sections.original: πρὸς τῆς θανούσης ἐμφανῶς κάκιστος ὤν .
sections.translation: by my dead wife . Now , since thou hast dared this loathly crime , come , look thy father in the face . Art thou the man who dost with gods consort , as one above the vulgar herd ? art thou the chaste and sinless saint ?

sections.label: Section 950
sections.original: οὐκ ἂν πιθοίμην τοῖσι σοῖς κόμποις ἐγὼ
sections.translation: Thy boasts will never persuade me to be guilty of attributing ignorance to gods . Go then , vaunt thyself , and drive1 [*] thy petty trade in viands formed of lifeless food ; take Orpheus for thy chief and go a-revelling , with all honour for the vapourings of many a written scroll ,

sections.label: Section 955
sections.original: ἐπεί γ ’ ἐλήφθης . τοὺς δὲ τοιούτους ἐγὼ
sections.translation: seeing thou now art caught . Let all beware , I say , of such hypocrites ! who hunt their prey with fine words , and all the while are scheming villainy . She is dead ; dost think that this will save thee ? Why this convicts thee more than all , abandoned wretch !

sections.label: Section 960
sections.original: ποῖοι γὰρ ὅρκοι κρείσσονες , τίνες λόγοι
sections.translation: What oaths , what pleas can outweigh this letter , so that thou shouldst ’ scape thy doom ? Thou wilt assert she hated thee , that ’ twixt the bastard and the true-born child nature has herself put war ; it seems then by thy showing she made a sorry bargain with her life ,

sections.label: Section 965
sections.original: εἰ δυσμενείᾳ σῇ τὰ φίλτατ ’ ὤλεσεν .
sections.translation: if to gratify her hate of thee she lost what most she prized . ’ Tis said , no doubt , that frailty finds no place in man but is innate in woman ; my experience is , young men are no more secure than women , whenso the Queen of Love excites a youthful breast ;

sections.label: Section 970
sections.original: τὸ δ ’ ἄρσεν αὐτοὺς ὠφελεῖ προσκείμενον .
sections.translation: although their sex comes in to help them . [*] Yet why do I thus bandy words with thee , when before me lies the corpse , to be the clearest witness ? Begone at once , an exile from this land , and ne ’ er set foot again in god-built Athens

sections.label: Section 975
sections.original: μήτ ’ εἰς ὅρους γῆς ἧς ἐμὸν κρατεῖ δόρυ .
sections.translation: nor in the confines of my dominion . For if I am tamely to submit to this treatment from such as thee , no more will Sinis , [*] robber of the Isthmus , bear me witness how I slew him , but say my boasts are idle , nor will those rocks Scironian , that fringe the sea ,

sections.label: Section 980
sections.original: φήσουσι πέτραι τοῖς κακοῖς μ ’ εἶναι βαρύν .
sections.translation: call me the miscreants ’ scourge .

sections.label: Section 981
sections.original: οὐκ οἶδ ’ ὅπως εἴποιμ ’ ἂν εὐτυχεῖν τινα
sections.translation: I know not how to call happy any child of man ; for that which was first has turned and now is last .

sections.label: Section 983
sections.original: πάτερ , μένος μὲν ξύστασίς τε σῶν φρενῶν
sections.translation: Father , thy wrath and the tension of thy mind are terrible ; yet this charge , specious though its arguments appear ,

sections.label: Section 985
sections.original: εἴ τις διαπτύξειεν , οὐ καλὸν τόδε .
sections.translation: becomes a calumny , if one lay it bare . Small skill have I in speaking to a crowd , but have a readier wit for comrades of mine own age and small companies . Yea , and this is as it should be ; for they , whom the wise despise , are better qualified to speak before a mob .

sections.label: Section 990
sections.original: ὅμως δ ’ ἀνάγκη , ξυμφορᾶς ἀφιγμένης ,
sections.translation: Yet am I constrained under the present circumstances to break silence . And at the outset will I take the point which formed the basis of thy stealthy attack on me , designed to put me out of court unheard ; dost see yon sun , this earth ? These do not contain ,

sections.label: Section 995
sections.original: οὐδ ’ ἢν σὺ μὴ φῇς , σωφρονέστερος γεγώς .
sections.translation: for all thou dost deny it , chastity surpassing mine . To reverence God I count the highest knowledge , and to adopt as friends not those who attempt injustice , but such as would blush to propose to their companions aught disgraceful or pleasure them by shameful services ;

sections.label: Section 1000
sections.original: οὐκ ἐγγελαστὴς τῶν ὁμιλούντων , πάτερ ,
sections.translation: to mock at friends is not my way , father , but I am still the same behind their backs as to their face . The very crime thou thinkest to catch me in , is just the one I am untainted with , for to this day have I kept me pure from women . Nor know I aught thereof , save what I hear

sections.label: Section 1005
sections.original: γραφῇ τε λεύσσων · οὐδὲ ταῦτα γὰρ σκοπεῖν
sections.translation: or see in pictures , for I have no wish to look even on these , so pure my virgin soul . I grant my claim to chastity may not convince thee ; well , ’ tis then for thee to show the way I was corrupted . Did this woman exceed in beauty

sections.label: Section 1010
sections.original: πασῶν γυναικῶν ; ἢ σὸν οἰκήσειν δόμον
sections.translation: all her sex ? Did I aspire to fill the husband ’ s place after thee and succeed to thy house ? [*] That surely would have made me out a fool , a creature void of sense . Thou wilt say , Your chaste man loves to lord it . No , no ! say I , sovereignty pleases only those

sections.label: Section 1015
sections.original: θνητῶν ὅσοισιν ἁνδάνει μοναρχία .
sections.translation: whose hearts are quite corrupt . Now , I would be the first and best at all the games in Hellas , but second in the state , for ever happy thus with the noblest for my friends . For there one may be happy , and the absence of danger

sections.label: Section 1020
sections.original: κρείσσω δίδωσι τῆς τυραννίδος χάριν .
sections.translation: gives a charm beyond all princely joys .

sections.label: Section 1021
sections.original: ἓν οὐ λέλεκται τῶν ἐμῶν , τὰ δ ’ ἄλλ ’ ἔχεις ·
sections.translation: One thing I have not said , the rest thou hast . Had I a witness to attest my purity , and were I pitted ’ gainst her still alive , facts would show thee on enquiry who the culprit was .

sections.label: Section 1025
sections.original: νῦν δ ’ ὅρκιόν σοι Ζῆνα καὶ πέδον χθονὸς
sections.translation: Now by Zeus , the god of oaths , and by the earth , whereon we stand , I swear to thee I never did lay hand upon thy wife nor would have wished to , or have harboured such a thought Slay me , ye gods ! rob me of name and honour , from home and city cast me forth , a wandering exile o ’ er the earth !

sections.label: Section 1030
sections.original: καὶ μήτε πόντος μήτε γῆ δέξαιτό μου
sections.translation: nor sea nor land receive my bones when I am dead , if I am such a miscreant ! I cannot say if she through fear destroyed herself , for more than this am I forbid . With her discretion took the place of chastity ,

sections.label: Section 1035
sections.original: ἡμεῖς δ ’ ἔχοντες οὐ καλῶς ἐχρώμεθα .
sections.translation: while I , though chaste , was not discreet in using this virtue . [*]

sections.label: Section 1036
sections.original: ἀρκοῦσαν εἶπας αἰτίας ἀποστροφήν ,
sections.translation: Thy oath by heaven , strong security , sufficiently refutes the charge .

sections.label: Section 1038
sections.original: ἆρ ’ οὐκ ἐπῳδὸς καὶ γόης πέφυχ ’ ὅδε ,
sections.translation: A wizard or magician must the fellow be , to think he can first flout me , his father ,

sections.label: Section 1040
sections.original: ψυχὴν κρατήσειν , τὸν τεκόντ ’ ἀτιμάσας ;
sections.translation: then by coolness master my resolve .

sections.label: Section 1041
sections.original: καὶ σοῦ γε ταὐτὰ κάρτα θαυμάζω , πάτερ ·
sections.translation: Father , thy part in this doth fill me with amaze ; wert thou my son and I thy sire , by heaven ! I would have slain , not let thee off with banishment , hadst thou presumed to violate my honour .

sections.label: Section 1045
sections.original: ὡς ἄξιον τόδ ’ εἶπας · οὐχ οὕτω θανῇ .
sections.translation: A just remark ! yet shalt thou not die by the sentence thine own lips pronounce upon thyself ; for death , that cometh in a moment , is an easy end for wretchedness . Nay , thou shalt be exiled from thy fatherland , and wandering to a foreign shore drag out a life of misery ;

sections.label: Section 1050
sections.original: μισθὸς γὰρ οὗτός ἐστιν ἀνδρὶ δυσσεβεῖ .
sections.translation: for such are the wages of sin . [*]

sections.label: Section 1051
sections.original: οἴμοι , τί δράσεις ; οὐδὲ μηνυτὴν χρόνον
sections.translation: Oh ! what wilt thou do ? Wilt thou banish me , without so much as waiting for Time ’ s evidence on my case ?

sections.label: Section 1053
sections.original: πέραν γε πόντου καὶ τόπων Ἀτλαντικῶν ,
sections.translation: Ay , beyond the sea , beyond the bounds of Atlas , if I could , so deeply do I hate thee .

sections.label: Section 1055
sections.original: οὐδ ’ ὅρκον οὐδὲ πίστιν οὐδὲ μάντεων
sections.translation: What ! banish me untried , without even testing my oath , the pledge I offer , or the voice of seers ?

sections.label: Section 1057
sections.original: ἡ δέλτος ἥδε κλῆρον οὐ δεδεγμένη
sections.translation: This letter here , though it bears no seers ’ signs , arraigns thy pledges ; as for birds that fly o ’ er our heads , a long farewell to them .

sections.label: Section 1060
sections.original: ὦ θεοί , τί δῆτα τοὐμὸν οὐ λύω στόμα ,
sections.translation: ( aside ). Great gods ! why do I not unlock my lips , seeing that I am ruined by you , the objects of my reverence ? No , I will not ; I should nowise persuade those whom I ought to , and in vain should break the oath I swore .

sections.label: Section 1064
sections.original: οἴμοι , τὸ σεμνὸν ὥς μ ’ ἀποκτενεῖ τὸ σόν .
sections.translation: Fie upon thee ! that solemn air of thine is more than I can bear .

sections.label: Section 1065
sections.original: οὐκ εἶ πατρῴας ἐκτὸς ὡς τάχιστα γῆς ;
sections.translation: Begone from thy native land forthwith !

sections.label: Section 1066
sections.original: ποῖ δῆθ ’ ὁ τλήμων τρέψομαι ; τίνος ξένων
sections.translation: Whither shall I turn ? Ah me ! whose friendly house will take me in , an exile on so grave a charge ?

sections.label: Section 1068
sections.original: ὅστις γυναικῶν λυμεῶνας ἥδεται
sections.translation: Seek one who loves to entertain as guests and partners in his crimes corrupters of men ’ s wives .

sections.label: Section 1070
sections.original: αἰαῖ , πρὸς ἧπαρ · δακρύων τ ’ ἐγγὺς τόδε ,
sections.translation: Ah me ! this wounds my heart and brings me nigh to tears to think that I should appear so vile , and thou believe me so .

sections.label: Section 1072
sections.original: τότε στενάζειν καὶ προγιγνώσκειν σ ’ ἐχρῆν
sections.translation: Thy tears and forethought had been more in season when thou didst presume to outrage thy father ’ s wife .

sections.label: Section 1074
sections.original: ὦ δώματ ’, εἴθε φθέγμα γηρύσαισθέ μοι
sections.translation: O house , I would thou couldst speak for me and witness if I am so vile !

sections.label: Section 1076
sections.original: ἐς τοὺς ἀφώνους μάρτυρας φεύγεις σοφῶς ·
sections.translation: Dost fly to speechless witnesses ? This deed , though it speaketh not , proves thy guilt clearly .

sections.label: Section 1078
sections.original: φεῦ ·
sections.translation: Alas ! Would I could stand and face myself , so should I weep to see the sorrows I endure .

sections.label: Section 1080
sections.original: πολλῷ γε μᾶλλον σαυτὸν ἤσκησας σέβειν
sections.translation: Ay , ’ tis thy character to honour thyself far more than reverence thy parents , as thou shouldst .

sections.label: Section 1082
sections.original: ὦ δυστάλαινα μῆτερ , ὦ πικραὶ γοναί ·
sections.translation: Unhappy mother ! son of sorrow ! Heaven keep all friends of mine from bastard birth !

sections.label: Section 1084
sections.original: οὐχ ἕλξετ ’ αὐτόν , δμῶες ; οὐκ ἀκούετε
sections.translation: Ho ! servants , drag him hence ! You heard
